entries.what_they_read: I saw 'Trade player tokens that move with real stats' up top, then the three-step breakdown explaining fair-value price versus market price, and the $100 play-money offer with 'no real money, no seed phrase' spelled out clearly.
entries.their_interpretation: Okay so this is basically a fantasy sports meets paper-trading app — they calculate what a player *should* be worth from stats, the crowd trades around that number, and you're hunting the gap between the two. That's actually a clean concept, I get it.
entries.their_decision: I'd click 'Claim $100' and see what the player cards look like.
entries.their_rationale: No seed phrase, no signup, no real money — the friction cost is basically zero, so the risk of clicking is nothing. And the gap-between-fair-value-and-market-price framing is exactly the kind of thing I think about anyway, so I'm curious whether their 'fair value' model is any good.
confusion_notes: 'The gap is your edge' — sounds clean but I don't yet know how the fair-value price is actually calculated; 'stats' is vague, could be points only or full advanced metrics
'Buying raises the price for the next person' — is this a bonding curve? They never say that word but it sounds like one, and I'd want to know before I trade seriously
'AWAITING TIP-OFF — Prices move after tonight's games' — does that mean tokens are frozen right now? Can I still place orders or am I just looking?

No explanation of what 'devnet' means or whether this is blockchain-backed at all — I own SOL so I noticed there's no wallet connect prompt, which is either intentional simplicity or they're hiding something
first_impression_elements: The 'no real money · no seed phrase' subtext under the Claim $100 button — that line did the most trust work for me immediately
The LeBron example with actual dollar figures ($5.59 fair value vs $4.20 market) — concrete numbers made the mechanic click fast
The 'AWAITING TIP-OFF' banner — it's the first thing I registered visually and made me wonder if the app was even live yet
ignored_elements: The '↓Terms explained' link — I saw it, assumed it was legal boilerplate, skipped it
The 'ThesisBeing right early — and staying right — pays better than being right once' line — felt like flavor text, didn't slow me down
The Feedback button — not relevant to me on a first visit

checkpoint.q1_what_am_i_buying: From what I can tell, I'm buying a token that represents a player — like a fake share of Kevin Durant or whoever. It's not a real asset, it's play money tied to how that player performs statistically. Think of it like a pretend stock where the company is the player's stat line.
checkpoint.q2_what_moves_price: Two things seem to be moving it — the stats-based fair value updates after real games, and then other users buying or selling pushes the market price around. So if a bunch of people pile into KD after a big game, the market price climbs even if the fair value hasn't caught up yet. That gap is exactly what I'm watching.
checkpoint.q3_hold_week: Honestly, the app hasn't told me explicitly what happens if I just sit on it. I'd assume the fair value keeps updating with each game, and the market price drifts based on what other users do — so my position either grows or shrinks depending on both. But I'm not 100% sure if there's any decay or expiration mechanic I'm missing.
